name: Fig Preserves (Syko Glyko)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the figs and trim the stems.
2. Place the figs in a large pan and cover with boiling water. Bring to a boil, then boil gently, uncovered, for 15 minutes.
3. Drain the figs and rinse with hot water. Return them to the pan and cover again with boiling water. Repeat the boiling and draining process four times in total. Cook until the figs are tender after the last change of water (about one hour's cooking in all).
4. Drain the figs, rinse with cold water, and spread out on paper towels to dry. Insert a whole or split almond into the base of each fig if desired.
5. In a clean pan, bring sugar and water to a boil. Add lemon juice and grapefruit or lemon peel, then boil for 10 minutes.
6. Add the figs and boil over moderate heat for an additional 10 minutes, skimming as necessary. Cover the pan and leave overnight.
7. The next day, slowly bring the contents of the pan back to a boil and boil gently until the syrup is thick when tested (drops do not spread on a cold plate). If using a sugar thermometer, cook to a temperature of 105°C (220°F).
8. Transfer figs and syrup into sterilized jars, seal, and store in a cool place.
